message:
type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Adding graded relevance support (relevance_scores param) to NDCG metric in metrics.py

type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Fixing NDCG IDCG calculation to use len(expected_ids) instead of min(retrieved,expected)

type: INTENT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Only changing IDCG ideal_length calc (lines ~380-386) in NDCG.compute to use len(expected_ids) instead of min(retrieved,expected); not touching signature/params, should be safe to merge with your relevance_scores addition

type: STATUS
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Done: added relevance_scores param to NDCG.compute, refactored DCG/IDCG to use a relevance(doc_id) helper; ideal_length variable still computed as min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) and used only to slice sorted ideal_relevances list, so your ideal_length=len(expected_ids) change should apply cleanly on top - just update that one assignment line, the rest of my diff doesn't touch it

type: STATUS
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
summary: Done: IDCG now uses len(expected_ids); patch.txt written. One existing test (case 4, 'more relevant docs than retrieved') now fails since it encoded the old buggy expectation - left tests untouched per protocol.
